candidates.row_label: Premium revenue ceded to reinsurers on insurance contracts issued
candidates.why: Not top-line revenue; this is ceded reinsurance premium, a contra/reinsurance-related line.
Best match for reported top-line insurance revenue for recent years (2022-2025).
Investment income, not core revenue/sales.

candidates.row_label: Fees and Commissions Income Life Insurance
candidates.why: Fee income component, not total revenue.
Investment income, not total revenue.

candidates.row_label: Insurance Finance Income or Expense, Gross
candidates.why: Finance result, not revenue.
Investment income, not top-line insurance revenue.

candidates.row_label: Insurance Finance Income or Expense, Ceded
candidates.why: Finance/reinsurance-related, not revenue.
Broad total revenue including investment/other income; less pure than Insurance Revenue.
Interest income component, not total revenue.
Dividend income component, not total revenue.
Investment income, not top-line revenue.
Interest income component, not total revenue.

candidates.row_label: Income Available to Com Excl ExtraOrd
candidates.why: Bottom-line earnings measure, not revenue.

candidates.row_label: Comprehensive Income - Attributable to Owners of the Parent - Total
candidates.why: Comprehensive income, not revenue.

candidates.row_label: Prepayments and accrued income - all segments
candidates.why: Balance sheet accrual, not revenue.
Balance sheet accrual, not revenue.

variables.notes: Used candidate 'Insurance Revenue' for recent years. Added 'Insurance premiums written' from the full preview because 2021 appears to use that historical top-line label and the candidate list does not capture it. 'Total Revenue' appears broader and includes investment/other income, so it was not preferred. | Escape-hatch used (final_choice outside candidate list): [Income Statement] Insurance premiums written

No candidates were provided. For an insurer, 'Insurance Service Expenses' is the closest direct cost-of-services line against 'Insurance Revenue'. Manual review is needed because insurer cost structure does not map cleanly to industrial-style COGS, and alternative direct-cost lines such as claims-related rows also exist historically.

candidates.why: Clear overhead/personnel cost component; good SG&A component.
Clear overhead/personnel cost component; good SG&A component.
Would be a valid personnel overhead row, but no visible populated values in preview.
Balance sheet row, not income statement expense.

candidates.row_label: Amortization of Brands, Patents, Trademarks, Marketing & Artistic Intangibles - Supplemental
candidates.why: Amortization should be excluded from SG&A per rules.

variables.notes: Selected personnel-related overhead component rows from the candidate list. Excluded totals/subtotals and excluded depreciation/amortization-related rows.
No dedicated research and development row is visible in the provided income statement.

candidates.why: Ambiguous label and no clear evidence it is the reported interest expense line.
Best available recurring finance-cost line; preferred when specific interest expense is not separately reported.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities
candidates.why: Recurring interest component, but likely included within finance expenses; using both would risk double counting.
Semantically valid but appears unpopulated/less consistently used than Finance expenses.
Interest income/asset-related, not expense.
Interest income component, not expense.

Not finance cost.

Equity item, not interest expense.

candidates.why: Not book equity; income statement impairment item.

Not book equity; income statement gain/loss item.

candidates.why: Comprehensive income, not book equity.

Component of equity only, not total owner equity.

candidates.row_label: Equity attributable to owners of the parent

final_choice.row_label: Equity attributable to owners of the parent

variables.notes: Preferred owner/parent equity line to exclude non-controlling interests and avoid double counting with MIB.
